description: Stage cli-c events goldens — events fanout + chains reports produced by runEventsFanout / runEventsChains with {deterministic:true}. Four golden files per main fixture: .fanout.human.txt, .fanout.json, .chains.human.txt, .chains.json. Plus scope=all variant (4 files) and maxDepth=1 chains variant (2 files) on their respective fixtures. The Rust engine must byte-match the .json goldens.
capturePoint: runEventsFanout({workspace, format, scope, deterministic:true, out}) and runEventsChains({workspace, format, scope, maxDepth, deterministic:true, out})
jsonEnvelope: JSON.stringify({al_sem_version, generated_at, kind, summary, entries|chains}, undefined, 2) — insertion-order keys, generated_at='0' under deterministic:true.
cycleNote: cycleDetected:true fires in walkEventChain when a subscriber routineId == a publisher routineId on the active path. In the current engine this requires the same AL procedure to have both [IntegrationEvent] (kind=event-publisher) and appear as subscriberRoutineId in an event edge. The routine-indexer assigns a single kind= (first-match wins), so this is not expressible via real AL source — it is only exercised in unit tests with mocked EventGraph data (test/engine/event-flow.test.ts). ws-event-pub-cycle documents the nearest real-AL structure (mutual subscriptions) but produces cyclesDetected=0.
